recommendations.rationale: The Leading cohort (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) leads on 1-month RS but every one has NEGATIVE 3-/6-month RS — the rotation is not yet confirmed structurally. If their 3-month RS climbs toward >0, the leadership handoff is durable and you lean further into cyclicals/defensives; if their 1-month RS rolls back under the benchmark while tech's structural RS holds, it was a 1-month head-fake and tech re-asserts. Estimate from RRG trajectory, not advice.
recommendations.structuralAnchor: Leading sectors' 3-/6-month RS still negative — rotation tactically real, structurally unconfirmed.
recommendations.invalidation: the Leading cohort's 1-month RS rolling back under the benchmark = head-fake, tech re-leads.

events.event: 30-year bond auction — RESULT (today's long-end supply test, PASSED)
events.consensus: RESOLVED 1:00pm ET (web-VERIFIED this run, U.S. Treasury / Investing.com): the $22B 30-year reopening stopped at a 5.058% high yield vs 5.050% at the prior auction — a hair soft (yield edged up ~1bp) but NO tail. Bid-to-cover not disclosed in public summaries (confirm at Treasury). The tape held/extended GREEN through it (S&P +0.70% on the day, VIX ~16), so the modal 'solid auction' path largely played out.

scenarios.name: weak (tail) — DID NOT happen
scenarios.expectedEffect: prior-window estimate, now RESOLVED: no tail materialized — long-end yields did not spike and the long-duration complex was not pressured into the close

scenarios.name: on-the-screws / solid — CLOSEST to outcome
scenarios.expectedEffect: REALIZED: 5.058% vs 5.050% prior with no tail = roughly on-the-screws to a hair soft; low-impact, the bull held near highs into the close and defers the macro decision to next week's CPI cluster

scenarios.name: strong (stops through) — partial
scenarios.expectedEffect: prior-window estimate: yield edged UP not down, so not a strong stop; the risk-on green close came from the broad tape, not a duration bid

events.psychologyNote: RESOLVED — carried scenario odds are shown for the record; the realized outcome sat between 'solid' and 'a hair soft.' The clean auction removes the immediate long-end risk into the close and hands the next decision to CPI (7/14). No single-name straddle ⇒ impliedMovePct null.

events.event: June CPI — the near-term fork for an extended tape
events.consensus: 8:30am ET Tue 7/14 (BLS, VERIFIED this run). Headline/core consensus ~+0.2-0.3% m/m is an ESTIMATE — confirm live. The headline-CPI tail is EASING as crude/energy fades (XLE 1m RS the worst, −7.1). Now lands under a hawkish-hold Fed (7/28-29 prices ~79-89% hold / ~14-21% hike, no 2026 cuts) — so a hot print has more bite. Also the Q2 big-bank earnings kickoff week — XLF read-through.

scenarios.name: hot (core hotter than est.)
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: SPX ~ -1 to -2%, yields up, RE-ARMS the hike tail (cut-odds already near zero); the catalyst a complacent, extended tape is UNHEDGED for; sector: re-fires the long-duration de-rate (XLK/QQQ/VGT), entrenches the financials/defensives lean; name: worst for the AI-semis and deepens MSFT's downtrend

scenarios.name: in-line
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: modest relief; the structural bull holds near highs, the fresh rotation persists tactically, tech's structural RS lead intact

scenarios.name: cool (softer than est.)
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: SPX ~ +1 to +2%, yields ease, softens the hike tail; the duration bid that extends the tech-led bull to new highs; supportive even for a MSFT relief bounce

events.psychologyNote: Estimate — probs 28/46/26: the fading oil tail (XLE −7.1 = crude-fade proxy) removes the headline upward bias, so in-line is modal. The key asymmetry is SHARPER under a hawkish-hold Fed: a HOT print doesn't just delay cuts (none are priced) — it re-arms the ~14-21% HIKE tail into a market at ATH with VIX 16 that is complacent about it. That is where the cheap hedge pays. No single-name straddle ⇒ index reaction bands. Consensus unverified vs a fresh fetch this run — re-pull live.

Consumer-demand read landing the session before OPEX; control-group feeds the Q2 GDP nowcast. XLY Weakening (1m RS −0.59, tangled, −0.35% below its 200-day) — a soft print confirms the consumer wobble.

scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: growth-positive but yields-up in a rate-sensitive tape at highs — mixed; sector: XLY firmer off a weak base, but firmer yields cap the long-duration relief

events.consensus: SPY put OI concentrated ~740 (STALE — 7/6; re-pull the chain). Spot 751.02 sits AT the STALE ~750 call wall.

scenarios.name: pin >740
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: positive-gamma compression into expiry — supported by the low VIX (16.05) and the tape near highs — IF CPI/PPI/retail don't break the level first

scenarios.name: break <740
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: put support rolls off → vol/direction release; negative-gamma waterfall risk, amplified if a hot CPI (7/14) already pushed spot down from the ATH

events.psychologyNote: Estimate. Gamma pins hardest into OPEX; the levels are STALE (7/6) with spot now at the old call wall — the wall has likely MIGRATED up with the rally, so re-pull. Pin-prob nudged to 0.58 (VIX low/falling, tape near highs = calm-pinning base case) — offset by the dense pre-expiry cluster (CPI/PPI/retail) any of which can break the level first.

events.event: FOMC decision + MSFT FY26 Q4 print (same day)
events.consensus: FOMC decision 2:00pm ET (Jul 28-29 meeting, VERIFIED). Path VERIFIED this run (CME FedWatch / prediction markets): ~79-89% HOLD, ~2-8% cut, ~14-21% HIKE; Fed funds 3.50-3.75%, NO cuts projected for 2026, Chair Warsh hawkish ('inflation remains elevated'). MSFT after the close: rev ~$86.7-87.8B, Q4 OM ~44.5-45.6% (consensus/seasonality est.). Implied move ~6% is STALE (7/6 straddle) — refresh live.

scenarios.name: beat (MSFT) / dovish-hold (Fed)
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: mild QQQ/VGT lift + a duration bid; name: the structural-repair path for MSFT — a reclaim toward the 50-day (~404) and beyond; sector: eases the Tech read

scenarios.name: inline / as-expected
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: neutral; name: MSFT drifts — the FY27 capex guide sets the tone; sector: rotation thesis unchanged

scenarios.name: miss (MSFT) / hawkish (Fed)
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: adds to the de-gross; name: MSFT downtrend extends, retests lower; sector: deepens the software-lag / AI-capex 'show-me' discount and risks SPREADING to the semis

events.psychologyNote: Estimate. RARE double-catalyst — the FOMC decision (2pm) and the MSFT after-hours print collide Jul 29. MSFT is STRUCTURALLY broken (bear-stack, −30% off its high) so it is an event trade, not a momentum long; a miss shaded ~+3-5pts vs option-implied for the sell-the-news asymmetry in a show-me AI-capex tape — its structural downtrend reinforces that lean. Straddle ~6% STALE — refresh.

psychology.read: REGIME (named): a calm, positive-gamma, COMPLACENT risk-on bull near all-time highs, closing GREEN today (S&P +0.70%, web-verified) — SPY bull-stacked, −0.87% off its 52-week high, +8.75% over a rising 200-day, VIX 16.04 falling for a 5th read. This is STRUCTURALLY a bull; the composite score reads 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable this run (VIX-only). Into the close, today's long-end supply test PASSED (30y stopped 5.058% vs 5.050% prior, no tail) and the tape held green — no fresh reason to de-risk overnight. Two features sit ON TOP of the frame without changing it: (1) a FRESH, still-shallow leadership rotation — financials/defensives (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) Lead on 1-month RS but their 3-/6-month RS are NEGATIVE, so tech (XLK 3m RS +20) remains the structural RS leader; today's broad-over-tech tape (S&P +0.70% > Nasdaq +0.20%) corroborated the lean for ONE session (the XLK 1m-RS wobble oscillated −0.59→−0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82 = noise, fails the persistence gate); and (2) one genuine structural CRACK — MSFT is bear-stacked, −29% off its high and −13.7% below its 200-day, the AI-capex 'show-me' de-rate at the single-name level, but so far IDIOSYNCRATIC (the AI-semis VGT/SPMO are bull-stacked near highs). CROWD POSTURE: complacent and short-vol into a KNOWN dense catalyst cluster (CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29), now under a verified HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (no 2026 cuts, a ~14-21% hike tail) that gives a hot CPI more bite; chasing the 1-month sector rotation; and having left MSFT for dead. FALSIFIERS of this read: (1) SPY losing its 50-day (~739) = first structural crack; (2) MSFT's weakness SPREADING to QQQ/VGT (idiosyncratic → systematic) = the real topping tell; (3) VIX inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0); (4) a HOT CPI (7/14) that re-arms the hike tail and breaks the OPEX level. Net posture into the close: STAY LONG and HOLD overnight, carry a CHEAP hedge into the CPI cluster, trade the fresh rotation as a bounded swing (not a regime change), and do NOT treat MSFT's downtrend as a dip.
inputs: SPY 751.02 bull-stack (20>50>200), +1.6% over the 50-day (~739), +8.75% over the 200-day (~691), −0.87% off the 52-week high (~758); mom126/252 +9.1% / +22.4%; closed GREEN +0.70% today (web-verified)
Intraday tape web-verified: S&P +0.70% vs Nasdaq +0.20% = broad market outpacing tech, corroborating the non-tech lean for ONE session
Breadth structurally broad: 10 of 11 sectors bull-stacked; only XLC bear-stack (−4.0% below 200-day), XLY tangled (−0.35%)
Leadership FRESH not entrenched: XLF +5.33 / XLV +4.46 / XLI +2.98 / XLU +2.73 on 1-month RS, but ALL have negative 3-/6-month RS
Tech still the structural RS leader: XLK 3-month RS +19.95, 6-month +17.92, price +22.1% over its 200-day, despite a −0.82 1-month-RS wobble
Persistence gate: XLK 1-month RS oscillated −0.59→−0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82 across 5 reads = NOISE (no persistent direction), NOT a rotation signal
XLF 1-month RS holding a Leading +5.33 (ran 4.15→4.86→5.89→5.61→5.33) = a real swing signal, but 3m/6m RS negative = fresh, not structural
MSFT 380.79 BEAR-stack: −5.8% below the 50-day, −13.7% below the 200-day, mom126/252 −20.1% / −22.7%, −29.3% off its 52-week high
MSFT idiosyncratic so far: QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs (200d +13.7% / +19.2% / +21.8%) — the complex is NOT dragged down
VIX 16.04 — a PERSISTENT 5-read decline (16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04, ~−5% on the day) = deepening complacency; term structure carried contango (IVTS ~0.85)
30y auction RESOLVED (verified): stopped 5.058% vs 5.050% prior, no tail — the long-end test PASSED into the close
FOMC path VERIFIED (CME FedWatch): 7/28-29 ~79-89% HOLD / ~2-8% cut / ~14-21% HIKE; no 2026 cuts, Chair Warsh hawkish
ENERGY the tactical worst: XLE 1-month RS −7.1 (deteriorating), but 6-month RS +12.76 and +7.9% over its 200-day = crude-fade, not structural break

Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run (CNN bot-blocked) — sentiment band unknown, not assumed
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: STRUCTURE-FIRST, anti-reactive: the fresh Tier-A MA/long-horizon slice re-frames the whole book and BARELY moved run-over-run (SPY 750.69→751.02) — so the structural thesis is UNCHANGED, as it should be. The intraday XLK RS wobble stays a do-nothing (fails the persistence gate at 5 reads; tech is the structural leader). The financials rotation is a bounded SWING (negative 3m/6m RS), now with one session of broad-over-tech corroboration — not yet the gate. MSFT stays a top STRUCTURAL card — a downtrend, not a dip. Event odds: the 30y auction RESOLVED clean (no tail), so the immediate long-end risk is off the table and the pre-close posture is HOLD overnight. CPI kept 28/46/26 but the hot-print asymmetry is SHARPER under the verified hawkish-hold Fed (a hot core re-arms the ~14-21% hike tail, not merely delays cuts). OPEX pin 0.58 (VIX low, near highs) but levels STALE 7/6 with spot at the old call wall. MSFT miss shaded heavier for sell-the-news asymmetry in a show-me AI-capex tape. All labeled estimates seeded from FRESH Tier-A bars + web-verified calendar/auction/Fed path + STALE options (7/6) — re-seed the options/consensus live.

experimental.title: Mega-cap structural-breakdown vs index-at-highs divergence detector (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a top-weight name is in a structural DOWNTREND (bear-stack, below its 200-day, deeply off its 52-week high) WHILE the index / its own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (this run: MSFT −14% below its 200-day, −30% off its high, bear-stack; QQQ/VGT bull-stacked near highs). Quantify the divergence (name 200-day distance − ETF 200-day distance, plus the 52-week-range gap) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the name mean-revert UP to the index (idiosyncratic, buy the eventual 50-day reclaim) or does the index roll over TO the name (systematic top, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: the Tier-A structural slice (maStack, ma200Dist, pctFrom52wHigh) — now computed per name + the ETFs. This run it is the single most important structural read and the OLD brief missed it entirely. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Fresh-vs-entrenched rotation classifier: 1-month RS leading while 3/6-month RS lag (idea)
experimental.note: Method: classify a sector's leadership as FRESH (1-month RS positive/Leading but 3- and 6-month RS negative — this run: XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) vs ENTRENCHED (all three horizons positive — this run: none of the tactical leaders; tech is the structural RS leader). Base-rate the forward outcome: does a fresh rotation persist (3-month RS climbs >0 = durable handoff) or fade (1-month RS rolls back = head-fake)? Turns 'rotation' from a one-number snapshot into a horizon-aware, falsifiable call. Inputs: the Tier-A rsMom1m/3m/6m per sector (now emitted). Directly frames this run's 'trade the rotation as a swing, not a regime change.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Persistence-gated RS-change detector (noise vs signal) (idea)
experimental.note: Method: for each momentum/RS series, compute the tail consecutive-same-direction run across the last N brief-history snapshots (the rlbrief.js consecutiveRun/isPersistentSignal helpers) and only surface a 'change' when the run is ≥ minRun OR a structural level breaks. This run is a live proof: XLK 1m RS oscillated −0.59→−0.53→−0.94→−0.81 = a length-1 tail run = NOISE, so 'tech rotated out' is not actionable, whereas XLF's 4.15→4.86→5.89 is a length-2+ run = a real swing signal. Base-rate the hit-rate of persistence-gated vs raw single-slice signals to quantify how much whipsaw the gate removes. Inputs: brief-history.jsonl (have) + the pure helpers (have, selftest-covered). Promote to a visible 'signal quality' badge if recurring.

experimental.title: Complacency-into-catalyst gauge: persistent VIX decline + event density (idea)
experimental.note: Method: combine a PERSISTENT VIX decline (this run: 16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04, a 5-read fall) with a forward event-density count over the next ~10 trading days (this run: CPI/PPI/retail + OPEX + FOMC/MSFT = very dense) and the index's distance from its ATH (−0.87%) to score a 'priced-for-perfection' complacency reading — the setup where cheap protection has the best risk/reward. Base-rate forward drawdown conditioned on high-complacency-into-high-event-density. Inputs: ^VIX series + macroEvents[] + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh (all have). Frames this run's 'carry a cheap hedge' call. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns / realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole calm read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M (add VIX9D/3M to the Tier-A fetch). Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Hawkish-hold hike-tail asymmetry gauge: how a macro print transmits when cut-odds are ~zero (idea)
experimental.note: Method: condition the market's reaction function on the VERIFIED FOMC-priced path, not just the print's surprise. This run the 7/28-29 FOMC prices ~79-89% HOLD, only ~2-8% cut, and a LIVE ~14-21% HIKE tail (CME FedWatch, verified), with no 2026 cuts and a hawkish Chair. In that regime a hot CPI does NOT merely delay cuts (there are none to delay) — it RE-ARMS a hike tail, so the downside convexity of an inflation upside-surprise is larger than in a cut-path regime, even at the same surprise magnitude. Build a 'hike-tail bite' score = f(priced hike prob, priced cut prob, distance-to-ATH, VIX complacency) that scales the recommended hedge size ahead of a CPI/PPI. Base-rate forward SPX drawdown on hot-CPI prints split by whether the curve carried a live hike tail vs priced cuts. Inputs: CME FedWatch priced path (web, verify each run) + macroEvents[] + ^VIX + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh. Directly frames this run's sharper hot-CPI asymmetry. Promote if recurring — no owning tool prices the Fed-path-conditioned reaction function today.
